brand_dna.category: Female-led performance sportswear and running apparel for everyday athletes, sold DTC from the UK.
brand_dna.core_belief: More than a sportswear brand - they design sportswear to perform with you so you can achieve more than you thought possible, where meditation meets motion.
proof_points: Hoodies made from 50% organic cotton and 50% recycled cotton, 420gsm brushed American fleece
Sustainable materials like recycled polyester and organic cotton with ethical production
Featured in British Vogue and The Times
Running socks with Q-Skin material and 4-way stretch for moisture wicking
brand_dna.positioning: Premium, minimal and intention-led - refined running apparel that sits above high-street sportswear on price and design.

voice_pillars.name: More Than
voice_pillars.desc: Frames every product as bigger than sport - purpose, identity and possibility, not just performance specs.
voice_pillars.quote: FOR THE RUNNERS WHO DO MORE THAN RUN

voice_pillars.name: Intentional And Grounded
voice_pillars.desc: Calm, purposeful language about movement as a deliberate choice, blending stillness with effort.
voice_pillars.quote: still choose to move with intention

voice_pillars.name: Quietly Technical
voice_pillars.desc: Leads with engineered detail and refined feel rather than loud hype or gimmicks.
voice_pillars.quote: engineered with technical detailing and innovative fabrics

voice_pillars.name: Consciously Made
voice_pillars.desc: Ties performance to sustainability and ethical production without preaching.
voice_pillars.quote: push your body while protecting the planet

phrases: achieve more than you thought possible
the layer you forget you're wearing

icp: Everyday runners, largely women, who balance routine and responsibility, train with intent, and want premium, consciously made kit that looks refined on and off the run.

personas.name: The Intentional Runner
personas.description: A woman who trains with purpose around a busy life and is moved by messaging about movement as choice and self-definition.

personas.name: The Conscious Buyer
personas.description: A values-driven shopper who pays more for recycled and organic materials and ethical production, moved by the sustainability angle.

personas.name: The Design-Led Athlete
personas.description: Style-aware buyer who wants sportswear that reads premium and minimal, moved by technical detailing and the refined aesthetic.

offers: 10% off your first order for newsletter sign-up
Free UK Tracked 48 shipping on orders over £65
Free UK returns via prepaid label
Returns accepted within 30 days of delivery, unworn with tags
Free-of-charge size exchanges, subject to stock
International shipping to EU, US, Canada, Australia and New Zealand (rates at checkout)

allowed.claim: Hoodies are made from 50% organic cotton and 50% recycled cotton, 420gsm brushed American fleece.
allowed.source: MATHAN Hoodie and Emblem Hoodie product pages

allowed.claim: As seen in British Vogue and The Times.
Running socks use Q-Skin material with 4-way stretch for moisture wicking.
Free UK 48hr delivery on orders over £65 and free UK returns.
AeroLite Short Sleeve has strategically placed reflective branding to keep you visible from behind.

banned.claim: The best running kit in the world / #1 sportswear brand.
banned.why: FTC substantiation - unqualified superlative that cannot be proven.

banned.claim: 100% sustainable or eco-friendly with no qualification.
banned.why: FTC Green Guides - unqualified sustainability claim; materials are only part organic/recycled.

banned.claim: Guaranteed to improve your run times or performance.
banned.why: FTC substantiation - guaranteed results claim with no evidence.

banned.claim: Loved by thousands / hundreds of 5-star reviews.
banned.why: Invented review counts - actual product pages show only 2-3 reviews each.

banned.claim: Only a few left - selling out in hours (when untrue).
banned.why: Fake urgency - prohibited unless genuinely accurate stock scarcity.

banned.claim: Endorsed by British Vogue or The Times.
banned.why: Invented endorsement - 'as seen in' is editorial mention, not an endorsement.
